sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application 88 of 2016 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA 368/2016. 2. For hearing of main case. 04.11.2025 Mr. Faheem Ali Memon, advocate for respondent. This reference application is pending since 2016 without any significant progress. On the last date of hearing, the following order was passed: “Pursuant to last order Mr. Faheem Ali Memon, advocate is present and states that the judgment relied upon is perhaps not attracted to the facts and circumstances of the present case. He states that the matter is squarely covered by the judgment reported in 2002 PTD 1279. Learned counsel for the applicant may assist. To come up after four weeks.” Today, the applicant remains unrepresented without any intimation or justification; therefore, the instant reference application is dismissed for non- prosecution along with pending application(s). A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge M. Khan
